title: Ozrei N'takkilomandrife
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]], also known as Knife|[[Knife/Legends|knives as weapons]]}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Ozrei.jpg]] |name=Ozrei N'takkilomandrife |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]]{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Star Wars (1977) 91|''Star Wars'' (1977) 91]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black/blue]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue[[Star Wars (1977) 100|''Star Wars'' (1977) 100]] |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Nagai expeditionary force|Nagai fleet]][[Star Wars (1977) 103|''Star Wars'' (1977) 103]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I am a leader of my people, and a servant of their people. I love my world. For Nagi—for the Nagai—I would do as bad as I have done again, or worse, without regret or hesitation.|Ozrei N'takkilomandrife|[[Star Wars (1977) 107|''Star Wars'' (1977) 107]]}} '''Ozrei N'takkilomandrife''', also known as '''Knife''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] and one of the foremost leaders of his [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Nagai expeditionary force|invasion fleet]] during the [[Nagai–Tof War]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. He commanded several early missions into territory belonging to the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]. The son of the [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Krai H'voc]], Knife first encountered Alliance agents [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] when he tried to set up a [[Slavery/Legends|slave ring]] on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] using [[Chewbacca's family/Legends|Chewbacca's family]] as hostages to keep the native [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] in line. His efforts were [[Skirmish on Kashyyyk (Nagai invasion)|foiled]] by a Wookiee uprising, however. During the invasion, Knife learned of his half-brother [[Bey]] and immediately befriended him, not showing any sign of discrimination against the half-breed. Shortly after his activities on Kashyyyk, he led an [[Skirmish on Kabray|attack]] on the planet [[Kabray]], but he and his [[Commando/Legends|commando]] squad were captured by the Alliance in the process. After being taken to an Alliance base on the [[Forest/Legends|Forest]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Knife's [[Brain/Legends|brain]] was scanned by several [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] [[Hoojib/Legends|Hoojibs]], which revealed to the Alliance the full extent of the Nagai's invasion plans. As the Nagai [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Second Battle of Endor|attacked]], Knife was freed from his [[Prisoner/Legends|captivity]] by Bey, who was acting as a double agent. Not long after, the [[Tof/Legends|Tofs]]—old enemies of the Nagai—followed the Nagai fleet into the known [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and began to inflict devastating losses on them. Despite Knife's reservations, the Nagai joined forces with the Alliance to repulse the greater threat. Knife had a hard time adapting to the new allegiance, constantly picking fights with Alliance warriors. However, the allegiance proved fruitful, and they managed to [[Battle of Saijo|defeat]] the Tofs on the planet [[Saijo/Legends|Saijo]]. With the conflict over, Knife returned with the Nagai fleet to their conquered home planet of [[Nagi/Legends|Nagi]] to liberate it from the ruling Tofs. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|Did you know, I found out I have a little brother just about your age?|Bey, to Han Solo|}} [[File:Ozrei-SW91.jpg|thumb|left|120px|Knife lifts his glass to his allies.]] The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] Ozrei N'takkilomandrife was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] to a prominent Nagai [[Scout/Legends|scout]] named [[Krai H'voc]]. Ozrei advanced through the ranks of the Nagai military, eventually attaining the rank of [[Commander/Legends|commander]]. He was well known for his loyalty and efficiency, skills he utilized during the Nagai's struggle against their perpetual enemies, the [[Tof/Legends|Tof]]. Before Ozrei had even been born, and during the height of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] rule over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], his [[Parent/Legends|mother]], Krai, became the first Nagai to scout Imperial territory, residing on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] for a time. Krai found the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] ripe for conquest, as it was engaged in a fierce [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|civil war]]. After learning all she could about the galaxy and its people, Krai left Corellia and returned to her [[Species/Legends|species]], telling them to wait until the war had ended so that they could attack the weakened loser. During Krai's stay on Corellia, she gave birth to a half-breed son named [[Bey]] and abandoned him following her return to the Nagai. After Ozrei's birth and subsequent advancement into the Nagai military, Bey finally discovered his long-lost mother in the Unknown Regions, and met her other family, including Ozrei. When Ozrei heard of his half-brother, he welcomed him with open [[Limb/Legends|arms]], accepting him into the family. At the same time, news of the conclusion of the Galactic Civil War reached the Nagai people. The [[Time/Legends|time]] for their invasion had finally arrived. Ozrei was sent as an advance scout to enter the new territory and create a [[Slavery/Legends|slave trade]] to disrupt the newly forming government, the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]. Ozrei's new brother Bey was sent as well, to serve as a spy for the imminent arrival of the [[Nagai expeditionary force|Nagai fleet]]. ===The invasion begins=== {{Quote|That's really low, Knife. What kind of scum are you anyway?''"
"''Scum who is tired of the sound of your voice.|Han Solo and Commander Knife|}} [[File:Ozrei2.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Knife silences his Wookiee captive.]] Commander N'takkilomandrife was sent to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], where he quickly made friends with several [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] desperate for money. Because of the Wookiee's [[Shyriiwook/Legends|unique tongue]], they were unable to pronounce Ozrei's name, and they took to calling him "Knife" in Shyriiwook for his skills with his [[Tehk'la blade]]s. Knife formed a slavery ring headed by himself and the Wookiee [[Vargi]], ready to restart the business that had flourished under the Galactic Empire. Vargi was the brother of [[Mallatobuck/Legends|Mallatobuck]], who was herself the [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] of [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], a famous hero of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Vargi informed Knife of Chewbacca's importance to the new galactic government, and Knife made plans to ransom [[Chewbacca's family/Legends|Chewbacca's family]] and capture the mighty Wookiee, believing that it would do much to demoralize local resistance to the new slave trade. However, as soon as Knife had kidnapped Chewbacca's wife and his young son, [[Lumpawaroo/Legends|Lumpawarrump]], he was notified that the Wookiee had just arrived on the world in the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Cobra]]'' with Alliance [[General/Legends|Generals]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Knife took Vargi and met up with the companions at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], requesting that he and Vargi accompany them back to [[Chewbacca's home]] for drinks. However, when they arrived, Chewbacca became immediately suspicious, seeing that his family was not present. After the party entered the home, Wookiees under Knife's command entered with weapons trained on the Rebel heroes, with Chewbacca's family in tow. Knife informed the group that he would be selling them into slavery and took Chewbacca out into the city to parade his captive around. [[File:Ozrei3.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Knife is knocked unconscious during the Skirmish on Kabray.]] Unfortunately for Knife's plan, Solo and Calrissian were able to get away from their captors and incited a [[Skirmish on Kashyyyk (Nagai invasion)|riot]]. Chewbacca personally defeated Vargi in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|one-on-one combat]], and the Wookiees rose up against Knife's loyal allies. Assessing the situation, Knife decided to cut his losses and quickly retreated from the area. Knife found Calrissian's ship, the ''Cobra'', with its defense systems offline, and he commandeered it, speeding back to the Nagai. ===Further exploits=== {{Quote|So Knife got clean away…in a fast ship! Well you can count on one thing, Lando…I'm gonna track him down, and set up a little rematch, as soon as I can!|Han Solo|}} Although he had temporarily escaped a bad situation, Knife was back on the move. On [[Saijo/Legends|Saijo]], Knife hired a rebel named [[Durne]] to foster a [[Skirmish in the Cantros system|fight]] between two factions of Rebels in the [[Cantros system/Legends|Cantros system]]. Durne was highly successful, and the two sides, each believing that the other was working for the Empire, slowly wiped each other out. The catastrophe was only stopped when the Rebel heroes Han Solo, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] were able to reveal the deception.[[Star Wars (1977) 93|''Star Wars'' (1977) 93]] With several bases now established, the Nagai invasion intensified. Knife was put in charge of a task force of [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] charged with [[Skirmish on Kabray|attacking]] a diplomatic conference on [[Kabray]] and capturing several prominent [[Alliance of Free Planets|Alliance]] leaders. Knife and his team quickly took several Alliance members captive, including Solo and Organa, who were present at the meeting. Knife put the leaders in a contained [[Prison/Legends|cell]] until he could transport them back to his commanders. However, when Knife entered the cell, he was attacked by [[Jahn]], a [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] teenager who had been working under Organa. Jahn knocked Knife [[Sleep/Legends|unconscious]], and a fight broke out between the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] and Knife's men, resulting in a victory for the Alliance.[[Star Wars (1977) 95|''Star Wars'' (1977) 95]] [[File:Ozrei4.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Knife and his warriors are led away as captives of the Alliance.]] Knife and his team were taken captive following the attack. Zeltron teenagers who had taken part in the skirmish herded the group back to a ship to be taken to a holding facility on the Alliance base on the [[Forest/Legends|Forest]] [[Moon/Legends|Moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. As a prisoner on Endor, Knife had his [[Brain/Legends|brain]] scanned by [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] [[Hoojib/Legends|Hoojibs]]. The Hoojib operatives, led by [[Plif/Legends|Plif]],[[Star Wars (1977) 97|''Star Wars'' (1977) 97]] discovered information that the Nagai fleet was about to begin an attack on the Endor base. The Alliance tried to evacuate the forest moon, but they were too late, and a climactic [[Second Battle of Endor|battle]] began. ===The Second Battle of Endor=== {{Quote|Mercy to the enemy isn't our way, Bey. I thought you understood that.|Ozrei N'takkilomandrife|}} [[File:Ozrei6.jpg|thumb|left|110px|Knife is wounded by Han Solo.]] Meanwhile, Knife's half-brother Bey had been secretly passing the Alliance's battle plans to the Nagai leadership. Bey helped a group of Nagai make a secret landing on Endor, and they quickly released Knife and his men from their captivity. Knife took charge of the group along with an attack force of [[Maccabree]] warriors, and prepared to annihilate the Alliance's base while the majority of their forces were [[Space warfare/Legends|engaged]] in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] against the Nagai fleet. Knife commanded [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Hol B'shaki]] to begin the attack while he went to reunite with Bey. However, Knife found Bey locked in battle with Han Solo, who had discovered Bey's part in working for the Nagai. Bey's martial arts and overbearing presence eventually disarmed Solo and left him sprawling on the forest floor. However, Bey hesitated to execute Solo, showing mercy, a distinctly un-Nagai trait. Seeing that Bey was about to violate the strict Nagai code of honor, Knife stepped in, preparing to kill Solo himself. Unfortunately for Knife, Bey could not allow his brother to kill Solo, and he lunged towards him, just as he was about to take the shot. Knife was knocked off balance and turned his blaster on Bey instead. Knife declared Bey a traitor, but before he could pull the trigger, he received a blaster shot in the torso. As Knife clutched his deadly wound, Solo advanced on the Nagai, having stopped him in his tracks. However, Bey rushed to Knife's side and threatened to shoot Solo if he would not leave the scene. Solo left to return to the Endor base and help stop the Nagai offensive while Bey tended to Knife's wound. Bey was able to get his half-brother back to the Nagai fleet, but after Solo was able to reveal the Nagai's plans to Alliance command, the battle was lost and the Nagai retreated. ===Back to command=== {{Quote|My time is yours to command, sir… and may I say how good it is to see you up and fully recovered from your wound? We were all very worried, you know.''"
"''Ah, yes… my wound. You know, Den… it's amazing how much pain and injury a body can endure, yet continue to live… sometimes even against its own inclination.|Lieutenant Den Siva and Commander Ozrei N'takkilomandrife|}} [[File:Ozrei5.jpg|thumb|right|85px|Knife turns his weapon on his brother Bey.]] Knife was gravely injured by the blaster wound and was taken off of active duty for a time. As such, he was not present for the [[Battle of Iskalon (Nagai–Tof War)|Battle of Iskalon]], instead spending his time recovering aboard a Nagai [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. Upon his recovery, Knife took to interrogating captured Alliance [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], subjecting them to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] with his [[Knife/Legends|knives]]. Knife's return was just in time to hear that the Nagai's old enemies, the Tof, had finally tracked them to their new location. Knife received word of the Tof's slaughter of Nagai forces at the [[Battle of Trenwyth]], and awarded posthumous commendations to all the soldiers lost in the battle. However, the reports of every soldier being killed were premature, and it seemed that one Nagai [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] had survived. [[Wing Commander/Legends|Wing Commander]] [[Taikotelai Akikoma]] reported to Knife's ship to make his report on the battle. However, Tai revealed that he had only survived by receiving help from Alliance operatives. Knife condemned this action, declaring that accepting mercy was as bad as showing it, and was a violation of the Nagai code of honor. Knife hurled one of his Tehk'la blades deep into Tai's chest, killing him and teaching a powerful lesson to his subordinates. Although Knife's subordinate, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Den Siva]], was shocked at the action, Knife was unaffected and ordered Siva to begin the planned [[Battle of Zeltros|invasion of Zeltros]] immediately. Ultimately, the invasion went poorly, with the Tof arriving, catching the Nagai off-guard, and capturing several of them.[[Star Wars (1977) 105|''Star Wars'' (1977) 105]] Only with the help of Alliance members on the world were the Nagai force able to defeat the Tof. Lieutenant Siva realized that the Tof had now become the major threat and decided to begin negotiations for a possible alliance.[[Star Wars (1977) 106|''Star Wars'' (1977) 106]] Knife was upset with the turn of events, but his superiors accepted the alliance, creating a tentative truce. ===The Battle of Saijo=== {{Quote|Prince Sereno! You are now a prisoner of war! Surrender and tell your subjects to do likewise—in the name of Nagi—and the Alliance of Free Planets!|Ozrei N'takkilomandrife|}} [[File:Ozrei8.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Knife executes a Tof in Sereno's palace.]] With the Nagai and the Alliance of Free Planets now allied, the Tof were slowly pushed back, eventually being isolated on their forward base on Saijo. There, the Alliance began a type of siege [[Battle of Saijo|warfare]], slowly moving towards the palace of [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Sereno]], the Tof leader. Knife was stationed alongside Luke Skywalker, Den Siva, Han Solo, Leia Organa, and [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Fenn Shysa]], as well as Alliance operatives [[Dani/Legends|Dani]], [[Maggie]] and [[Trif (Human)|Trif]]. The diverse group eventually found themselves holed up in a destroyed Saijo settlement on the outskirts of the Tof-erected palace. While there, Knife proved a difficult ally, trying to incite a fight with Solo as well as insulting his fellow Nagai, Siva, for being weak-willed. Knife was concerned about what would happen following the conclusion of the battle, believing that the Alliance would betray them at the mission's end. However, Skywalker did much to quell Knife's fears. Eventually, Skywalker split the group up, taking only Siva, Solo, Knife, Organa, and Dani to infiltrate the palace. As they slowly approached the main Tof structure, Siva and Skywalker witnessed a group of Nagai prisoners being herded into the palace by their Tof captors. The group quickly captured the Tofs and decided to create a ruse to get into the palace. Threatening their Tof prisoners, they posed as captives and were able to enter Sereno's throne room. As soon as they arrived in the room, the group found Sereno in an audience with the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady]] [[Lumiya]]. The Tof prisoners could not continue with the deception in front of their leader; however, Knife executed a Tof before he could betray them and took Sereno captive, holding him at blasterpoint. As a fight broke out in the chamber, Lumiya [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] Knife with a blaster shot and tried to finish him off. However, Lumiya herself was shot by Leia Organa. Lumiya was able to regain the upper hand over Organa through use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], but she was outnumbered by her foes. The time she had spent subduing Organa gave Knife's brother time to come to their rescue, whereupon he disabled Lumiya with a blaster shot to the back. Knife was touched by his brother's actions, finally finding the strength to forgive him for his betrayal on Endor. [[File:Ozrei9.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Knife is wounded by Dark Lady Lumiya during the Battle of Saijo.]] ===After the war=== Knife joined Bey and the rest of the Alliance heroes in celebrating an end to the Tof threat, but he realized that his species' homeworld of [[Nagi/Legends|Nagi]] was still under Tof control. Knife accepted an alliance with Mandalore Fenn Shysa and his [[Mandalorian Supercommando|Supercommandos]] to continue the fight against the Nagai's sworn enemy. The Alliance of Free Planets offered the planet of Saijo to the Nagai as a potential new homeworld to rebuild and live on, as well as full membership in the new government. However, the majority of the Nagai, along with Knife, knew that they had a duty to their homeworld. The fleet departed with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] to fight the Tof back in their home [[Star system/Legends|system]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I need no one to speak for me Den…I think Solo knows, in his heart, that I am not the monster he would like to believe me.|Ozrei N'takkilomandrife|}} [[File:Ozrei10.jpg|thumb|left|Knife and Han Solo finally lay aside their differences at the end of the Nagai–Tof War.]] Ozrei N'takkilomandrife was a particularly brutal being, having no regrets for his actions as long as they served his strict moral code. Knife held no qualms about torturing an enemy or even killing a subordinate if he found them to be lacking the strength that should be shown by a Nagai. He was also fairly unforgiving, holding a deep hatred for Han Solo for foiling his plots on Kashyyyk and Kabray, making it difficult for him to work beside him during the final battle on Saijo. Holding such strong loyalty to his race, the betrayal of his half-brother Bey on Endor affected Knife deeply. During his slow recovery, he suffered from a bout of depression and even suggested to Den Siva that he had perhaps lost the will to live. However, his strong will pulled him through this period, although he still had a hard time forgiving Bey. Knife even began to distrust anyone who was not a full-[[Blood/Legends|blooded]] Nagai after the event. However, following the Battle of Saijo, Knife's mentality changed, finding respect for the members of the Alliance after they laid their lives on the line repeatedly to protect his own. He was even able to ask the Mandalorians for help in the Nagai's next campaign against the Tof. Knife was a strong-willed enemy, not succumbing to potentially deadly wounds that he sustained during the Battle of Endor, and quickly pressed himself back into active service after recovering. While Knife proved a formidable enemy, he was also a powerful ally, which he proved during the battle of Saijo, succeeding in capturing the Tof Prince and surviving Lumiya's assault, helping to end the Nagai–Tof War. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|We also saved a lot of lives. The killing is over now.''"
"''Is it? I hope so. But I'll feel a lot better if we can just find out who Durne was really working for.|Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa, pondering over the identity of Durne's employer|}} Knife was created by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] for use in [[Marvel Comics]]' ongoing [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. He first appeared in [[1985]]'s [[Star Wars (1977) 91|''Star Wars'' 91]], and was used throughout a storyline involving the Nagai invasion of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' galaxy. Afterward, Knife went unused until [[2000]], when he was referenced in [[Rich Handley]] and [[Joseph Bongiorno]]'s article ''[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series]]'', which, among other things revealed Knife's true name of Ozrei N'takkilomandrife. The article also named Knife's mother, [[Krai H'voc]], and confirmed that Knife was the person who had hired the traitor Durne in [[Star Wars (1977) 93|''Star Wars'' 93]], a mystery that had previously been unsolved. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Ozrei N'takkilomandrife}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 91|''Star Wars'' (1977) 91]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 91}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 93|''Star Wars'' (1977) 93]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 93}} {{Mo}} {{Ret}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 95|''Star Wars'' (1977) 95]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 95}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 97|''Star Wars'' (1977) 97]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 97}} {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 100|''Star Wars'' (1977) 100]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 100}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 103|''Star Wars'' (1977) 103]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 103}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 107|''Star Wars'' (1977) 107]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 107}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Ozrei N'takkilomandrife |es=Ozrei N'takkilomandrife |fi=Ozrei N'takkilomandrife }} {{DEFAULTSORT:N'takkilomandrife, Ozrei}} [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Nagai]] [[Category:Officers]]
